title: Level 138
wikitext:
__NOTOC__ {{Liminal Theme}} {{Rewrite|date=7/2/2026|The amount it has just makes it bad. A simple concept ruined by absolute zonespam, and writing which is verbose. The concepts are also overdone, just beacuse its carbon tunnels you dont have to put in carbon monoxide everywhere. And as it goes on, the less quality the sectors have, the first two are mostly fine besides verbosities, but then it just degrades. And half of what is in the survival guide can be told in the description, or have been told already. btw there are SPaG errors and the sentences are often clunky and should be phrased a lot more smoothly. the verbosity, the zone spam and the overexplaining makes the level feel like a chore to read. Staff, If it doesn't deserve deletion replace it with a Rewrite or Fix template. If it does, please move it to my sandbox}} {{Audio|file=Level_-4.mp3|align=center}} {{Blurbackground|file=Image Of Sector 1.jpg}} {{DISPLAYTITLE:Level 138: ''"Carbon Tunnels" ''}}
{{Image Banner|image=Image Of Sector 1.jpg|caption=
'''Level 138''' is the '''139th''' level of the Backrooms.
}}
{{Class|p1=3|p2=4|p3=2}}
----
''' Description '''
----
Labelled as "Carbon Tunnels," '''Level 138''' remains one of the more unexplored and possibly undocumented levels of the Backrooms, as its environmental hazards prove themselves as an obstacle against the proper documentation held in the level. In order to enter '''Level 138''' safely, one should bring any kind of self-protection(e.g., any kind of mask). The reason for this concern comes from the principal oddities of '''Level 138''', being the presence of carbon dioxide (CO2) interspersed throughout the level's atmosphere, behaving as the main danger in '''Level 138''', as well as a cluttered infestation of [[Carbonations]]. Upon entering '''Level 138''', one may find it difficult to maintain an accurate sense of direction. This is a result of the level's tendency to inexplicably shift and change; this phenomenon of altering tunnels often confuses and disorients wanderers. Tunnels branch off in all directions, occasionally leading to dead-ends or looping in on themselves. '''Level 138''' is littered with various objects, such as bricks scattered around the floors, food, construction tools and wooden barrels. Aside from these objects, little to no food and water are found in '''Level 138'''. '''Level 138''' is quieter than it’s neighbouring levels, with the only noise present being the constant gas leaks from the pipes and it’s natural ambience. The enclosed structure of the level causes the sounds to have a light echo — making the sound universally heard across the entire level, regardless of one's location. Other than this, the only other naturally occurring sound within  '''Level 138''' is the moderately loud rumbling of pipes, similar to what one would hear on [[Level 2]]. Despite '''Level 138''' appearing to be safe, the carbon dioxide gases that are present throughout the level is deadly, as it could suffocate one and cause respiratory problems, if inhaled too much. Additionally, higher concentrated carbon dioxide gases in certain sections of the level may create a thick blanket of fog, where one is unable to see through, thus making the level even more hazardous. The only other hazard in this level are the entities that roam across the level, and the very high temperature of the level, ranging from 35°C to 45°C As to a physical description to '''Level 138''' itself, the whole level is a long-abandoned industrial maintenance complex with multiple odd and strange aspects that make it unique. It has also been documented to feature 5 distinct sectorsThese sectors are not in chronological order, so one may entirely skip sectors. An example of this are Sector 1 may lead to Sector 5. Additionally, not all sectors has an entrance to each other, all of which have been designated as sectors 1-5 respectively, beginning with: =='''Sector 1: "The Carbonized Tunnels"'''== {{Blurimage|caption= ----One of the few known images of Sector 1. ----|file=Image Of Sector 1.jpg}} The first sector to '''Level 138''', colloquially known as "The Carbonized Tunnels," is comprised of a vast and complex network of interconnected tunnels and corridors, all with the characteristic of possessing a perfect cylindrical shape. The tunnels appear to be walled by uniformly spaced beige-colored bricks that appear to be eroded severely, with some being entirely absentAttempting to reach into the empty spaces the absence of these bricks leave do not seem to reveal anything of interest, nor do any attempts to shine a light source into said spaces really show anything particularly strange or off. or chipped off, thus making the sector mildly unstable. {{Blurimage|file=3725.jpeg|caption=Another image of '''Level 138''' in another angle, featuring a [[Entity 9|Faceling]].}} The floor that supports the tunnels is made out of damp, weathered, and cracked concrete, with mold and algae growing along its edges, along with multiple potholes or oddly jagged sections dotting its endless surface. The upper portion of the tunnels possesses an inconsistently placed rectangular fluorescent ceiling lamps that loudly emit a constant buzz similar to [[Level 0]]’s lighting, rarely flickering or, in some occasions, permanently turning off. Scattered throughout the sector are black and rusted metal piping contrasting against the system of lights. These pipes are occasionally heavily rusted and/or in low quality, leading them to leak various hazardous gases. Upon further examination, suspicious and possibly hazardous gases that loosely resemble carbon dioxide seem to emerge from the piping and through the walls. =='''Sector 2: "Flooded Waterways"'''== {{Blurimage|file=139 Section 2.jpg|caption=An image of Section 2, area is completely flooded.}} The second sector of '''Level 138''' is a mass array of hallways that repeats in a pattern, alongside the flooded of carbonized murky water. The lights do not work, leaving the entire sector in perpetual darkness. It is speculated that [[Hydrolitis Plague|The Hydrolitis Plague]] to be present in these waters. Since this sector has been discovered well enough, previous wanderers have set up directions and ropes above the water to help wanderers navigate the sector. It is recommended to not stay in the carbonated water for extended periods of time, as there is a risk of getting a plague, irritations to the skin or the risk of inhaling excess carbon dioxide. Once one has traveled far enough, they will find themselves a ventilation shaft; In which one must go in to continue their journey. =='''Sector 3: "The Vents"'''== {{Blurimage|file=Image Of Sector 2.jpg|caption=An image of Section 3.}} Section 3 is an expansive, cramped ventilation system. It is composed of a shiny material made by a mixture of steel and iron, where a blue hue is present at the walls of the vents, providing a calming sensation that reduces stress and anxiety. The Sector is much cleaner than the other sectors in '''Level 138''', with little to no sendiments and dirt present in the area. Fans are present all throughout the sector giving a cool, calming breeze for one; along with releasing oxygen for one to breath and filtering out carbon dioxide. Fluorescent lighting consistently light the area, leading navigation to be easier, though they occasionally flicker or shut down entirely. '''Section 3''' is widely regarded as the safest section within '''Level 138''', due to it not possessing carbon dioxide, [[Entity 23|Carbonations]], [[Entity 9|Facelings]] and has minimal environmental hazards; serving as an optimal travel tool for one. The only hazard is the claustrophobic size of '''Section 3''', along with the non-euclidean properties throughout '''Level 138''', causing navigation and movement to be challenging. One may enter/exit the section by entering/exiting through a grayish vent grate. =='''Sector 4: The Subterranean Tiles"'''== {{Blurimage|file=139section4.gif|caption=The first known video recording of the Subterranean Tiles.|hovertext=The initial photographer apologizes for the low quality.}} Section 4, colloquially known as the Subterranean Tiles, is a volatile section of Level 138. This area's distinguishing feature compared to the other sections of the level is the walls, which are comprised of an infinite plane of white tiles of uniform measures. The floor is made up of a similar method, but the tiles are distinctly not consistent in size and a dark blue in color. The ceiling is an unknown reflective surface that displays everything in a dark-blue shade not dissimilar to that of the floor. Hemispherical lights, similar to ones located in pools, line the top of the walls at the junction near the ceiling and walls. The base of these lights is covered in a thin concrete mold. The lights are incredibly warm but do not dissipate heat, meaning one will not recognize its overwhelming temperature unless actively reaching for the light. The Subterranean Tiles are in reality a system of different hallways connected by a network of pitch-black staircases at either end of the hallway that go both up and down. These hallways appear to reach a peak and base, as at a certain point the staircases will not go further up or down respectively, becoming black-painted walkways instead. {{Blurimage|file=Lighting139.png|caption=A close-up of a light from Section 4, taken in [[Level 330]] to nullify its effects.}}The first hazard of the level are not physical, but are rather psychological. The lights of the level have been proven to have an adverse psychological effect on wanderers exposed to them in any capacity within the levelIf the lights are detached, the effects of the lights persist throughout other sections but cease upon exiting, and become benign excluding heat even if brought back within the level.. This psychological effect manifests as a persistent uncomfortable feeling, coupled with headaches and TinnitusTinnitus is a condition when a wanderer hears a ringing sound or a different variety of sounds when no corresponding external sound is present and that other people cannot hear. . These symptoms will progressively become worse, which is often paired with the onset of anxiety and the presence of general bodily aches. These effects are unfortunately fatal after an estimated three days of exposure, at which point all bodily functions will suddenly cease due to unknown causes. This can be avoided by finding exits as soon as possible.
The second primary hazard is indeed physical and is identified through a distinct presence of toxic Carbon Dioxide (CO₂). This gas is mixed with an unknown and untested concentration of Iodine gas. While Iodine gas is typically formed via sublimating, this gas appears here anomalously with no apparent source. While this does make it significantly more harmful to ingest, it turns it visible as a deep purple gas and forces it to linger in one place. The gas seems to clump together, acting as a blockage to many tunnels. While the gas can be easily avoided, it is by far the most fatal of the hazards within this section. This is because the gas is extremely potent and moves frequently from tunnel to tunnel in a process known as Clumped Migration. Safe spots for this phenomenon are the occasional broom closet door that appears at least once in every hallway, as the gas does not move under the door. =='''Sector 5: Riddle Shafts"'''== {{Blurimage|file=111111.jpeg|caption=An image of Section 5.}} The fifth sector to '''Level 138''', colloquially known as "Riddle Shafts" is comprised of a vast and complex network of interconnected mineshafts and caves, possessing an inconsistent and jagged terrain — including varying potholes and tight spaces. The caves are entirely made out of black, carbon and asphalt rocks with carbon dioxide reserves being present throughout the sector. The structure of the sector is inconsistent; as walls of carbon rocks may collapse on its own due to the pressure from the carbon dioxide reserves. The Asphalt Mines include various objects: such as pipes, rails, minecarts and mining equipments which are scattered throughout the sector. These objects are covered in black carbon dust, and are in low quality — often breakable and unusable. Fluorescent lighting line up perfectly, brightly lighting up the area, and vents may occasionally appear throughout the area releasing oxygen — though appear seldomly throughout the sector. The section is widely regarded as the most dangerous sector within '''Level 138''', as the sectors has the highest concentration of carbon dioxide due to the massive carbon dioxide reserves found throughout the sector. These carbon dioxide gases may suffocate one if inhaled too much, and may clump together to form a grayish thick fog that obstructs one’s view. The sector is heavily infested with [[Entity 23|Carbonations]], which makes ther sector even more dangerous. Due to this, it is highly recommended to wear a Carbon Mask to protect one from the harmful carbon dioxide gases. As such, the sector is the largest and the most unexplored sector of '''Level 138'''. =='''Entities'''== '''Level 138''' includes [[Entity 9|Facelings]] and [[Entity 23|Carbonations]] throughout all the sectors except for Sector 3, though they vary by concentration depending on various sectors. It is theorized that [[Hydrolitis Plague]] is present throughout Sector 2, though this remains unconfirmed. =='''Colonies/Outposts'''== Due to the environmental hazards of '''Level 138''', one cannot create colonies and outposts. There are several attempts of establishing colonies and outposts in '''Level 138''', though they all failed due to various reasons. =='''Survival Guide'''==
Addendum: "Tips For Survival"
Survival within Level 138 depends almost entirely on preparation, awareness, and restraint. The primary threat is not the entities, but the environment itself—specifically the pervasive presence of carbon dioxide and extreme heat. Before entering, it is strongly advised to equip a reliable breathing apparatus, such as a Carbon Mask. Without it, prolonged exposure will lead to dizziness, confusion, and eventual suffocation. Light clothing that resists heat while covering the skin is also recommended, as temperatures can quietly escalate to dangerous levels. Navigation is one of the most consistent causes of death in this level. The tunnels shift unpredictably, often looping or branching into misleading paths. Marking your route is essential; use chalk, string, or physical objects to track your movement. However, be aware that environmental changes may erase or displace these markers. When disoriented, remain calm and avoid rushing—panic leads to faster breathing, which increases carbon dioxide intake and accelerates exhaustion. Sound travels unnaturally well throughout the level due to its enclosed structure. Use this to your advantage. The constant hissing of gas leaks and distant pipe rumbling can help you determine safer or more dangerous areas. Louder gas activity often correlates with higher concentrations of CO₂. Additionally, areas with reduced sound intensity may indicate better airflow or proximity to safer zones such as ventilation systems. Each sector requires a different survival approach. In Sector 1, avoid unstable walls and leaking pipes; never linger near visible gas emissions. In Sector 2, minimize contact with the flooded water—move quickly and use any pre-existing ropes or guidance systems. In Sector 3, take advantage of the clean air and relative safety to rest, recover, and plan your next move. This sector should be treated as a temporary sanctuary, not a permanent refuge. Extreme caution is required in Sector 4, where psychological hazards become lethal over time. Avoid direct exposure to the lights as much as possible, and do not remain in this sector longer than necessary. If symptoms such as ringing in the ears or persistent discomfort begin, prioritize immediate escape. In addition, always monitor the movement of visible gas clusters; these can block routes or trap you if ignored. Sector 5 should be considered a last resort. The density of carbon dioxide, structural instability, and high entity presence make it exceptionally dangerous. Movement should be slow and deliberate, with constant attention to the ground and surrounding walls. If visibility becomes impaired by thick fog, stop moving until the air clears or you risk falling into unseen hazards. Encounters with entities such as Facelings are generally avoidable if not provoked. Maintain distance and do not initiate interaction. Carbonations, however, are far more dangerous due to their environmental integration—avoid any suspicious gaseous formations or moving fog, as these may not be natural. Finally, resource management is critical. Food and water are scarce, and overexertion will deplete your reserves rapidly. Conserve energy whenever possible, especially in high-temperature areas. Rest only in locations with confirmed airflow, such as vents or areas with reduced gas presence.
=='''Entrances'''== '''Level 138''' has numerous notable entrances: * A black waterslide in [[Level 71]] may lead to '''Level 138'''; * One may occasionally be transported to '''Level 138''' in [[Level 137]]; * Noclipping through a floor in [[Level 21]] may rarely lead here; * A blue door in [[Level 718]] may lead to '''Level 138'''; * Sleeping through the floor for 3 hours in [[Level 283]] leads to '''Level 138'''. * Finding a fogged-up darkened mirror in [[Level 305]] and interacting with it leads to '''Level 138'''. =='''Exits'''== '''Level 138''' has numerous notable exits: * A door found in Section 4 or 5 may lead back to [[Level 2]]; * Doorways scattered through '''Level 138’s''' deeper sectors may transition to [[Level 199]]; * Various factors in '''Level 138’s''' further sectors may transition into [[Level 140]] * A ladder leading to the rooftop of '''Level 138''' may lead to [[Level 27]] or [[Level 28]]. * Finding a pitch-black watertight exit door in Section 2 and entering will lead one to [[Level 49]]. {{Redirect|Prev=137|Next=139}} {{Collapse|initial=
